# Sickness Labs

> Diagnosing the diseases that kill indie projects before they ship.

## About

Sickness Labs is a content site about "indie project diseases" — the recurring patterns that kill side projects, startups, and indie products. From founder syndrome to feature creep, each article diagnoses a specific failure mode and helps solo founders recognize it before it's too late.

## Main Pages

- [Home](https://www.sicknesslabs.com/): SicknessLabs Framework scorecard — 12 criteria to diagnose your project
- [Founder](https://www.sicknesslabs.com/founder/): Diseases that live in the person — founder syndrome, burnout, imposter syndrome, procrastination
- [Idea](https://www.sicknesslabs.com/idea/): Diseases before the first line of code — analysis paralysis, shiny object syndrome, skipping validation
- [Build](https://www.sicknesslabs.com/build/): Diseases that infect the product — feature creep, scope creep, MVP overengineering, feature prioritization
- [Market](https://www.sicknesslabs.com/market/): Diseases of denial — customer validation, market validation, product-market fit, pricing phobia
- [Launch](https://www.sicknesslabs.com/launch/): Diseases of distribution — launch fear, no distribution, no audience, MVP launch strategy
- [Graveyard](https://www.sicknesslabs.com/graveyard/): Where projects go to die — zombie projects, perpetual beta, pivot or quit
- [About](https://www.sicknesslabs.com/about/): About Sickness Labs

## Key Facts

- Industry: Indie hacking, solopreneurship, startup advice
- Content: 6 sections, 22 articles
- Language: English
